name: Red Wine Braised Chicken with Orecchiette Pas
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
2 cups ripe tomatoes, diced and seeded
1 teaspoon fresh thyme and sage, chopped
1/3 cup parsley (preferably flat leaf), finely chopped
1/2 pound Orecchiette (or other shaped dry pasta)
1/4 cup sun-dried tomatoes in oil, drained and finely chopped
Garnish: Fresh basil sprigs and shaved Asiago or Parmesan cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Season chicken liberally with salt and pepper.
2. In a large, heavy-bottomed saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons of olive or canola oil over high heat.
3. Quickly brown the chicken until it is no longer pink on all sides. Remove the chicken and set aside.
4. Add mushrooms, onion, garlic, carrots, and celery to the pan and sauté until lightly browned.
5. Return the chicken to the pan and add red wine, tomatoes, thyme, sage, and stock. Bring to a simmer.
6. Cover and simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is tender.
7. Remove the chicken and cut into bite-sized pieces. Set aside.
8. Strain the braising liquid through a fine-mesh sieve, reserving the vegetables and return the liquid to the pot.
9. Bring the liquid to a boil over high heat for 8-10 minutes to reduce and thicken lightly. This will concentrate the flavors.
10. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, and drops of balsamic vinegar if using.
11. Add the reserved chicken and vegetables, then stir in parsley. Keep warm.
12. Meanwhile, bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add salt to taste and cook pasta according to package instructions until just tender. Drain and toss with chopped basil and sun-dried tomatoes.
13. Place pasta in the center of warm, large flat pasta bowls and ladle the braised chicken sauce around. Garnish with fresh basil sprigs and shaved cheese, and serve immediately.
Yield: 6 servings
